description: We promote competition and protect consumers with a clear end goal – economic growth and household prosperity. CMA is a non-ministerial department.

details.body:
We promote competition and protect consumers with a clear end goal – economic growth and household prosperity.

CMA is a non-ministerial department.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Information on your rights, what to do if things go wrong, and how to tell the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) about consumer problems.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Help for consumers

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Information on growing your business, selling products or services, and engaging with the Competition and Markets Authority.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Guidance for businesses

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Information and guidance for people who advise businesses on competition and consumer issues.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Information for advisers

ordered_featured_links.title: Report a problem to the CMA
Find information and guidance about mergers

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The Chair of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) works with the board to:

  • provide visible and strong leadership and develop a positive culture for the board and organisation as a whole
  • set the overall strategic direction of the CMA
  • represent the CMA to our partners, stakeholders, Ministers, Parliament and the public

The Chair of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) works with the board to:

  • provide visible and strong leadership and develop a positive culture for the board and organisation as a whole
  • set the overall strategic direction of the CMA
  • represent the CMA to our partners, stakeholders, Ministers, Parliament and the public

details.body:
The Chief Executive of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing visible and dynamic leadership to the CMA
  • driving performance in the organisation to ensure it meets its strategic priorities and delivers value for money
  • working with the chairman to represent the CMA to our partners, stakeholders, ministers, Parliament and the public

role_appointments.title: Sarah Cardell - Chief Executive of the Competition and Markets Authority

details.body:
The Interim Chief Executive of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing visible and dynamic leadership to the CMA
  • driving performance in the organisation to ensure it meets its strategic priorities and delivers value for money
  • working with the chairman to represent the CMA to our partners, stakeholders, ministers, Parliament and the public

The General Counsel at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • ensuring that the CMA’s legal work is of high quality consistently
  • providing guidance on the most important cases and acting as a strong advocate for competition, building relationships across the legal sector in the UK and internationally

Board members are responsible for:

  • setting the organisation’s strategic direction and policy framework
  • developing priorities
  • monitoring performance against its objectives
  • making decisions on Market Investigation References

role_appointments.title: Martin Coleman - Non-Executive Director and Panel Chair

role.title: Chair of the Office for the Internal Market Panel

role_appointments.title: Murdoch MacLennan - Non-Executive Director and Chair

details.body:
Non-Executive Directors of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) Board play an important role in setting up the organisation, working with the chair and chief executive.

As board members, they are also responsible for:

  • setting the organisation’s strategic direction and policy framework
  • developing priorities
  • monitoring performance against its objectives
  • making decisions on Market Investigation References

The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Secretary of State the overarching strategy for delivering Ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the Ministry to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Justice Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Sir Suma Chakrabarti - Permanent Secretary, Ministry of Justice

BPDTS Ltd is a digital technology company that’s been set up to provide specialist digital technology services to the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP).

BPDTS’s board sets out the strategy and agrees how operational decisions are taken.

details.body: The Executive Director for Digital Markets is directly responsible for the implementation of a new digital markets regime being introduced through the passage of the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Bill; covering regulation, compliance and enforcement for firms designated with strategic market status.

role_appointments.title: Will Hayter - Executive Director for Digital Markets

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for a range of professional disciplines in the Corporate Services directorate and for:

  • working with the Chief Executive to deliver cross-organisational capability to ensure the CMA achieves its strategic objectives
  • developing the CMA’s business services
  • providing organisational leadership across a range of functions to ensure that the Corporate Services directorate provides a first class service in a demanding and high-profile environment

Non-Executive Directors of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) Board play an important role in setting up the organisation, working with the chair and chief executive.

As board members, they are also responsible for:

  • setting the organisation’s strategic direction and policy framework
  • developing priorities
  • monitoring performance against its objectives
  • making decisions on Market Investigation References
A Non-executive Board Member is responsible for constructively challenging, and providing guidance and support to, the Executive Board.

The HM Land Registry Board Chair works closely with the Chief Executive and Chief Land Registrar in leading the development of the organisation. The role has responsibility for ensuring that proper governance practices are followed and performance is monitored effectively.

The Interim Executive Director for Competition Enforcement leads the Competition Enforcement function with the aim of tackling unlawful and damaging anti-competitive conduct for the benefit of people, businesses and the wider economy.

role.title: Interim Executive Director for Competition Enforcement

role_appointments.title: Juliette Enser -  Interim Executive Director for Competition Enforcement

details.body: The Executive Director for Competition Enforcement leads the Competition Enforcement function with the aim of tackling unlawful and damaging anti-competitive conduct for the benefit of people, businesses and the wider economy.

role_appointments.title: Juliette Enser - Executive Director for Competition Enforcement

details.body:
The Chief Strategy & External Affairs Officer is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • working with the Board to develop the CMA’s strategy
  • providing advice to government under the CMA’s advocacy function
  • leading the CMA’s external communications and relations

role_appointments.title: Jessica Lennard - Chief Strategy & External Affairs Officer

details.body:
The General Counsel at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • ensuring that the CMA’s legal work is of high quality consistently
  • providing guidance on the most important cases and acting as a strong advocate for competition, building relationships across the legal sector in the UK and internationally

The Interim General Counsel at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • ensuring that the CMA’s legal work is of high quality consistently
  • providing guidance on the most important cases and acting as a strong advocate for competition, building relationships across the legal sector in the UK and internationally

The Chief Data, Technology and Insight Officer is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation

  • overseeing the CMA’s full portfolio of work related to data, technology and analytics, including the work of its DaTA Unit, programmes on Artificial Intelligence and Online Choice Architecture, as well as the CMA’s internal technology infrastructure, systems and capabilities

  • analysis of emerging technologies, how these shape markets, and the implications for competition and consumer protection

  • leading the CMA’s digital transformation through technology, data and analytics

role.title: Chief Data, Technology and Insight Officer

details.body:
The Executive Director of Markets and Mergers at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible and dynamic leadership of the CMA’s mergers and markets work
  • managing associated work streams to provide value to markets and consumers

The Chief Operating Officer at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for a range of professional disciplines in the Corporate Services directorate and for:

  • working with the Chief Executive to deliver cross-organisational capability to ensure the CMA achieves its strategic objectives
  • developing the CMA’s business services
  • providing organisational leadership across a range of functions to ensure that the Corporate Services directorate provides a first class service in a demanding and high-profile environment

The Chief Economic Adviser at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • ensuring that the CMA’s economists provide high-quality advice, giving guidance on the most complex cases and developing centres of expertise

The General Counsel at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • ensuring that the CMA’s legal work is of high quality consistently
  • providing guidance on the most important cases and acting as a strong advocate for competition, building relationships across the legal sector in the UK and internationally

The Executive Director in Enforcement at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • leading the CMA’s enforcement work on Competition Act cases, criminal cartels and consumer enforcement, to provide value to markets and consumers
  • providing strong, visible and dynamic leadership of the CMA’s enforcement work

The Acting Executive Director in Enforcement at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • leading the CMA’s enforcement work on Competition Act cases, criminal cartels and consumer enforcement, to provide value to markets and consumers
  • providing strong, visible and dynamic leadership of the CMA’s enforcement work

The Executive Director in Enforcement at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • leading the CMA’s enforcement work on Competition Act cases, criminal cartels and consumer enforcement, to provide value to markets and consumers
  • providing strong, visible and dynamic leadership of the CMA’s enforcement work

The Acting Chief Executive of the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is responsible for:

  • establishing and managing the organisation
  • working with the chairman, the board and the executive team

ordered_roles.title: Acting Chief Executive of the Competition and Markets Authority

  • ensuring delivery of the CMA’s people strategy
  • working with CMA leaders, managers and all colleagues to ensure the best possible employee experience and that the CMA is a great place to work

details.body: Digital experts are responsible for providing advice and analysis to the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) on matters relating to its ongoing programme of digital casework, whilst also supporting preparatory work for the proposed new digital markets regime.

The Chief Strategy & External Affairs Officer is responsible for:

  • providing strong, visible leadership across the organisation
  • working with the Board to develop the CMA’s strategy
  • providing advice to government under the CMA’s advocacy function
  • leading the CMA’s external communications and relations

The Executive Director for Digital Markets is directly responsible for the implementation of a new digital markets regime being introduced through the passage of the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Bill; covering regulation, compliance and enforcement for firms designated with strategic market status.

details.body: The Interim Executive Director for Competition Enforcement leads the Competition Enforcement function with the aim of tackling unlawful and damaging anti-competitive conduct for the benefit of people, businesses and the wider economy.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Executive Director for Competition Enforcement

details.body:
The Interim Executive Director for Consumer Protection and Markets leads the Competition and Markets Authority’s (CMA) consumer protection and markets functions, including the enforcement of consumer protection law and delivering projects including:

  • market reviews
  • studies
  • investigations

that address broader features of markets that may not be working well for people, businesses and the wider economy.

This role also leads the CMA’s regulatory appeal function, through which we provide independent expert scrutiny of decisions by sector regulators.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Executive Director for Consumer Protection and Markets
